name: Synthetic Demographic Analogs Are Diagnostic Fairness Stress-test Material
statement: Walsh et al. use 100 original Casper responses and 100 analogous synthetic demographic-injected responses as an early fairness stress-test for open-response automated scoring, supporting synthetic variants as diagnostic probes rather than subgroup validation evidence.
population_or_context: Open-response SJT automated scoring for Casper admissions assessment responses.
time_horizon: Frontiers in Education article published on 2026-04-28 and retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: The fairness pilot uses 100 original and 100 analogous synthetic responses and reports near-zero average score deltas for inspected demographic comparisons.
caveats: The authors frame the fairness analysis as an early pilot.
Small sample size limits between-group effect-size interpretation.
SJT admissions responses are adjacent to, but not identical with, classroom student responses or correctness-scored constructed responses.
evaluation_method: Paired comparison of automated LLM evaluations on original responses and analogous demographic-injected synthetic responses.
reported_metrics: Average score deltas for gender and race comparisons; broader study reports automated-scoring performance against human ratings.
limitations: Not evidence that synthetic demographic variants can replace real subgroup fairness validation.
Included as bounded stress-test evidence from an adjacent open-response assessment context.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the original-versus-analogous synthetic response fairness pilot and its sample-size caveat.
